arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with rapid7/metasploit-framework (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with zynon/LdapRelayScan (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with swisskyrepo/PayloadsAllTheThings (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web', 'ad'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web', 'ad'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web', 'ad'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web, ad] in the exploit category. Fits when the engagement needs: This is a unified repository for different Metasploit Framework payloads, which merges these repositories:.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: This is a unified repository for different Metasploit Framework payloads, which merges these repositories:.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `mkdir -p ~/.msf4/payloads`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd ~/metasploit-payloads`; `ln -s ~/metasploit-payloads/python/meterpreter/ext_server_stdapi.py ~/.msf4/payloads/meterpreter/ext_server_stdapi.py`. This is a unified repository for different Metasploit Framework payloads, which merges these repositories:.

name: metasploit-payloads
summary: This is a unified repository for different Metasploit Framework payloads, which merges these repositories:

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need exploit, use metasploit-payloads because This is a unified repository for different Metasploit Framework payloads, which merges these repositories:.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `mkdir -p ~/.msf4/payloads` as documented upstream for metasploit-payloads.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `cd ~/metasploit-payloads` as documented upstream for metasploit-payloads.

When you need a concrete invocation, run `ln -s ~/metasploit-payloads/python/meterpreter/ext_server_stdapi.py ~/.msf4/payloads/meterpreter/ext_server_stdapi.py` as documented upstream for metasploit-payloads.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `file ~/.msf4/payloads/meterpreter/ext_server_stdapi.py` as documented upstream for metasploit-payloads.
When chaining exploit tools, pair metasploit-payloads with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `metasploit-payloads --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
